| 0596  | throttle                       | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A time-binding word; it changes when an action may proceed.                                                                                                                             |
| 0597  | tick                           | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A coordination word; it manages concurrency, interruption, or flow.                                                                                                                     |
| 0598  | timeout                        | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | Waiting is not free and patience must have a boundary. A timeout is a contract with reality about how long uncertainty is allowed to persist.                                           |
| 0599  | timeslice                      | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A coordination word; it manages concurrency, interruption, or flow.                                                                                                                     |
| 0600  | trigger                        | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A tempo-word; it governs order, waiting, branching, or repetition.                                                                                                                      |
| 0601  | try                            | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A coordination word; it manages concurrency, interruption, or flow.                                                                                                                     |
| 0602  | unblock                        | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A time-binding word; it changes when an action may proceed.                                                                                                                             |
| 0603  | until                          | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A tempo-word; it governs order, waiting, branching, or repetition.                                                                                                                      |
| 0604  | wait                           | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A sequencing word; it decides what happens next and under what condition.                                                                                                               |
| 0605  | wake                           | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A tempo-word; it governs order, waiting, branching, or repetition.                                                                                                                      |
| 0606  | when                           | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A time-binding word; it changes when an action may proceed.                                                                                                                             |
| 0607  | while                          | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A sequencing word; it decides what happens next and under what condition.                                                                                                               |
| 0608  | yield                          | Control Flow, Coordination, and Temporal Logic                 | A coordination word; it manages concurrency, interruption, or flow.                                                                                                                     |
| 0609  | address space                  | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0610  | aliasing                       | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0611  | allocation                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0612  | allocator                      | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0613  | arena                          | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0614  | borrow                         | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0615  | cache                          | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | Keep likely things close so future cost falls. Shadow: stale truth delivered at machine speed.                                                                                          |
| 0616  | cache line                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0617  | call frame                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0618  | call stack                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0619  | closure environment            | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0620  | compaction                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0621  | copy-on-write                  | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0622  | core dump                      | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0623  | deallocation                   | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0624  | destructor                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0625  | dynamic allocation             | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0626  | eager evaluation               | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0627  | escape analysis                | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0628  | execution context              | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0629  | finalizer                      | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0630  | frame pointer                  | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0631  | garbage collection             | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A reclamation word; unreachable memory is gathered and returned.                                                                                                                        |
| 0632  | generation                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0633  | handle                         | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0634  | heap                           | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0635  | heap object                    | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0636  | hot path                       | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0637  | inline cache                   | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0638  | interpreter loop               | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0639  | JIT                            | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A just-in-time word; translation happens at runtime rather than earlier in the forge.                                                                                                   |
| 0640  | layout                         | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0641  | lazy evaluation                | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0642  | leak                           | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0643  | lifetime                       | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0644  | live set                       | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0645  | locality                       | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0646  | lock-free                      | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.                                                                                                                       |
| 0647  | mark phase                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0648  | memory                         | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0649  | memory barrier                 | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An ordering word; it forbids certain reorderings across threads or hardware boundaries.                                                                                                 |
| 0650  | memory map                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0651  | memory pool                    | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0652  | memory safety                  | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0653  | move semantics                 | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0654  | object header                  | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0655  | page                           | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0656  | page fault                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0657  | pinning                        | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0658  | pointer                        | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A location word; it does not hold the thing, but where the thing may be found.                                                                                                          |
| 0659  | pool allocator                 | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0660  | prefetch                       | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0661  | quiescence                     | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0662  | reclaim                        | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0663  | reference count                | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0664  | register allocation            | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0665  | root set                       | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0666  | runtime                        | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living word; the conditions under which the program presently exists and executes.                                                                                                    |
| 0667  | sandbox                        | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A confinement word; actions are permitted only within a restricted space.                                                                                                               |
| 0668  | shadow stack                   | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0669  | slab allocator                 | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0670  | stack                          | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A layering word; order matters and the most recent thing is nearest the top.                                                                                                            |
| 0671  | stack frame                    | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0672  | stop-the-world                 | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0673  | sweep phase                    | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0674  | tail call                      | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0675  | thread-local storage           | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0676  | TLB                            | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0677  | tracing collector              | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0678  | unbox                          | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0679  | user heap                      | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A living-system word; it describes the program while it is actually in motion.                                                                                                          |
| 0680  | virtual memory                 | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0681  | VM                             | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A runtime-law word; it constrains performance, allocation, or safety at execution time.                                                                                                 |
| 0682  | wait-free                      | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | An embodiment-word; it concerns where computation resides and how it is reclaimed.                                                                                                      |
| 0683  | write barrier                  | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | A vessel-word; it shapes how memory and execution are physically arranged.                                                                                                              |
| 0684  | zero-copy                      | Runtime, Memory, and Execution                                 | Data movement stripped down to the minimum number of duplications. Use it when the bottleneck is physical movement rather than arithmetic.                                              |
| 0685  | boot                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0686  | bootloader                     | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0687  | capability mode                | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0688  | cgroup                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0689  | chroot                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0690  | context switch                 | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0691  | cron                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0692  | daemon                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0693  | device                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0694  | device tree                    | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0695  | direct I/O                     | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0696  | driver                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A translation word between software intent and device behavior.                                                                                                                         |
| 0697  | epoll                          | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0698  | exec                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0699  | exit code                      | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0700  | file                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0701  | file descriptor                | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0702  | file lock                      | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0703  | filesystem                     | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0704  | firmware                       | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0705  | fork                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0706  | futex                          | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0707  | group id                       | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0708  | init                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0709  | inode                          | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0710  | interrupt                      | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A priority word from the machine; ordinary flow is preempted for urgent handling.                                                                                                       |
| 0711  | ioctl                          | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0712  | IRQ                            | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0713  | isolate                        | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0714  | kernel                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A sovereignty word; the operating system's core authority over process, memory, and device.                                                                                             |
| 0715  | kernelspace                    | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0716  | kill signal                    | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0717  | link layer device              | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0718  | load average                   | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0719  | login shell                    | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0720  | mount                          | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0721  | namespace                      | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0722  | niceness                       | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0723  | nonblocking I/O                | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0724  | open file table                | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0725  | page cache                     | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0726  | page table                     | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0727  | path                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0728  | permission                     | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0729  | pipe                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0730  | poll                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0731  | privilege                      | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0732  | process                        | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A sovereignty word; an executing program with its own protected address space.                                                                                                          |
| 0733  | process table                  | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0734  | pseudo-terminal                | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0735  | pthread                        | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0736  | quota                          | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0737  | read-only mount                | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0738  | real-time scheduler            | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0739  | run queue                      | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0740  | scheduler class                | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0741  | select                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0742  | service manager                | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0743  | session leader                 | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0744  | shared memory                  | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0745  | signal                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0746  | socket                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A conduit word; a live endpoint for network communication.                                                                                                                              |
| 0747  | softirq                        | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0748  | spawn                          | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0749  | standard error                 | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0750  | standard input                 | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0751  | standard output                | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0752  | sticky bit                     | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0753  | stream descriptor              | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0754  | swap                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0755  | syscall                        | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0756  | system call boundary           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0757  | systemd unit                   | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0758  | tempfile                       | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0759  | terminal                       | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0760  | thread                         | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A line-of-execution word that shares process memory with its siblings.                                                                                                                  |
| 0761  | timer                          | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0762  | trap                           | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | An operating word; it changes how software inhabits the host machine.                                                                                                                   |
| 0763  | tty                            | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0764  | umask                          | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0765  | unmount                        | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0766  | user id                        | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0767  | userland                       | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A machine-host word; it marks a boundary where software meets system authority.                                                                                                         |
| 0768  | virtual file system            | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0769  | watchdog                       | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A kernel-adjacent word; it concerns process, device, file, or privilege.                                                                                                                |
| 0770  | writeback                      | Systems Programming and Operating-System Words                 | A host-word; it governs how programs bargain with the operating system.                                                                                                                 |
| 0771  | accept                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0772  | acknowledgment                 | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0773  | anycast                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0774  | API request                    | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0775  | API response                   | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0776  | availability zone              | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0777  | backoff                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0778  | backpressure                   | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | Resistance pushed upstream when demand outruns capacity. Without it, overload spreads faster than truth.                                                                                |
| 0779  | bandwidth                      | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0780  | bind                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A fastening word; it joins a name, port, address, or variable to a concrete referent.                                                                                                   |
| 0781  | broadcast                      | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0782  | broker                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0783  | CDN                            | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0784  | certificate                    | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A delegated-trust word; it lets one party assert another's identity by signed authority.                                                                                                |
| 0785  | checksum handoff               | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0786  | client                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0787  | client timeout                 | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0788  | cluster                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-body word; several machines are treated as one operational unit.                                                                                                                 |
| 0789  | connect                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0790  | connection pool                | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0791  | consensus                      | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine law-word; it creates shared order without a single unquestioned body.                                                                                                    |
| 0792  | consistency level              | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0793  | control plane                  | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0794  | CRC                            | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0795  | datagram                       | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0796  | deadline                       | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0797  | destination                    | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0798  | discovery                      | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0799  | DNS                            | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0800  | DNS record                     | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0801  | edge                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0802  | egress                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0803  | election                       | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0804  | endpoint                       | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0805  | eventual consistency           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | Agreement postponed rather than denied. Shadow: stale reads treated as if they were fresh truth.                                                                                        |
| 0806  | failover                       | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A continuity word; authority or traffic moves when the current holder fails.                                                                                                            |
| 0807  | fan-out                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0808  | firewall                       | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0809  | follower                       | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0810  | forwarding                     | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0811  | frame                          | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0812  | gateway                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0813  | gossip                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A rumor-propagation word; state spreads by repeated local exchange.                                                                                                                     |
| 0814  | gRPC                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0815  | handshake                      | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A mutual-recognition word used to establish parameters, trust, or shared readiness.                                                                                                     |
| 0816  | health check                   | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0817  | heartbeat                      | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0818  | hop                            | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0819  | HTTP                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0820  | HTTPS                          | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0821  | idempotency key                | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0822  | ingress                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0823  | jitter                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A timing-noise word; repeated operations arrive with unstable spacing.                                                                                                                  |
| 0824  | keepalive                      | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0825  | latency                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | The delay before useful work reaches the caller. Latency is what users feel even when throughput graphs look flattering.                                                                |
| 0826  | leader                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0827  | lease                          | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A temporary-authority word; a resource is held only for a bounded duration.                                                                                                             |
| 0828  | load balancer                  | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0829  | loss                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0830  | mailbox                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0831  | membership                     | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0832  | mesh                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0833  | message queue                  | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0834  | mTLS                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0835  | multicast                      | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0836  | NAT                            | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0837  | network partition              | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0838  | node                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0839  | origin                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0840  | packet                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0841  | packet loss                    | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0842  | peer                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0843  | pipeline depth                 | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0844  | port                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A contact word; a defined point of communication or adaptation.                                                                                                                         |
| 0845  | proxy                          | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A stand-in word; one body speaks or receives on behalf of another.                                                                                                                      |
| 0846  | publish-subscribe              | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0847  | QUIC                           | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0848  | quorum                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | Not everyone must agree, but enough must. This word turns distributed action into a threshold question instead of a unanimous fantasy.                                                  |
| 0849  | rate limit                     | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0850  | receiver window                | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0851  | redirect                       | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0852  | replica                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A mirroring word; state is maintained in more than one place.                                                                                                                           |
| 0853  | replication                    | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0854  | request                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0855  | request coalescing             | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0856  | response                       | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A returning word; the answer carried back across an invoked boundary.                                                                                                                   |
| 0857  | retry                          | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A second-attempt word; failure does not end the ritual if policy allows another try.                                                                                                    |
| 0858  | reverse proxy                  | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0859  | route                          | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A path word; messages or requests are sent by a chosen way rather than all ways.                                                                                                        |
| 0860  | router                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0861  | routing table                  | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0862  | RPC                            | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0863  | segment                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0864  | sequence number                | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0865  | service discovery              | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0866  | service mesh                   | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A side-channel governance word for service-to-service traffic.                                                                                                                          |
| 0867  | shard                          | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A splitting word; one logical body is divided across multiple partitions.                                                                                                               |
