| 0868  | sidecar                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0869  | sliding window                 | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A many-machine word; it coordinates transport, routing, or agreement beyond one process.                                                                                                |
| 0870  | socket                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A conduit word; a live endpoint for network communication.                                                                                                                              |
| 0871  | source                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0872  | split-brain                    | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0873  | stream                         | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A flow word; values arrive over time rather than all at once.                                                                                                                           |
| 0874  | strong consistency             | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A sameness word across distance; readers do not see old worlds after new ones commit.                                                                                                   |
| 0875  | subscription                   | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0876  | TCP                            | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0877  | timeout                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A patience-bound word; after a duration, waiting is no longer allowed.                                                                                                                  |
| 0878  | TLS                            | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0879  | topic                          | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0880  | tracing header                 | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0881  | UDP                            | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A packet-and-possibility word; it matters because the other side may be absent, slow, or stale.                                                                                         |
| 0882  | unicast                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0883  | virtual IP                     | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0884  | webhook                        | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0885  | WebSocket                      | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distributed-systems word; it manages failure, ordering, or reachability across nodes.                                                                                                 |
| 0886  | wire format                    | Networking and Distributed Systems                             | A distance-word; it carries intention across boundaries and unreliable space.                                                                                                           |
| 0887  | ACID                           | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0888  | append-only log                | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0889  | archive table                  | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0890  | audit trail                    | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0891  | B-tree                         | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0892  | backup                         | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0893  | base table                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0894  | bitmap index                   | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0895  | checkpoint                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0896  | column                         | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0897  | column store                   | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0898  | commit                         | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | The moment a proposed change becomes durable history. Shadow: writing too early, before the world is actually ready to carry the new truth.                                             |
| 0899  | compaction                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0900  | conflict                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0901  | constraint                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A bounding word; it removes degrees of freedom in order to improve correctness.                                                                                                         |
| 0902  | covering index                 | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0903  | cursor                         | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0904  | data lake                      | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0905  | data warehouse                 | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0906  | database                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0907  | deadlock                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A binding curse; multiple actors wait on one another forever.                                                                                                                           |
| 0908  | denormalization                | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0909  | dimension table                | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0910  | durability                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0911  | durable log                    | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0912  | entity table                   | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0913  | event store                    | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0914  | fact table                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0915  | failover replica               | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0916  | foreign key                    | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0917  | full table scan                | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0918  | index                          | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A retrieval word; it makes later access faster by storing an alternate path to data.                                                                                                    |
| 0919  | index hint                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0920  | index seek                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0921  | isolation level                | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0922  | join                           | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A union word; separate streams, sets, or histories are brought into one result.                                                                                                         |
| 0923  | keyspace                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0924  | LSM tree                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0925  | materialized view              | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0926  | migration                      | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | Stored reality changing shape without being lost. Good migrations are staged, inspectable, and reversible long enough to discover what the data actually contains.                      |
| 0927  | MVCC                           | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0928  | nonclustered index             | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0929  | OLAP                           | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0930  | OLTP                           | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0931  | partition                      | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0932  | partition key                  | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0933  | primary key                    | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0934  | query plan                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0935  | read committed                 | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0936  | read replica                   | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0937  | record lock                    | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0938  | redo log                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0939  | relation                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0940  | repeatable read                | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0941  | replication lag                | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0942  | retention                      | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0943  | rollback                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | Permission to retreat toward a previously trusted state. A system without rollback mistakes hope for safety.                                                                            |
| 0944  | row                            | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0945  | row store                      | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0946  | savepoint                      | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0947  | scan                           | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0948  | schema                         | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A shape word for stored meaning.                                                                                                                                                        |
| 0949  | secondary index                | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0950  | seed data                      | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0951  | serializable                   | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0952  | sharding                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0953  | snapshot isolation             | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0954  | sort key                       | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0955  | stored procedure               | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0956  | table                          | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0957  | table scan                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0958  | tenant isolation               | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0959  | transaction                    | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | An all-or-nothing persistence word.                                                                                                                                                     |
| 0960  | TTL                            | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0961  | tuple                          | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                                       |
| 0962  | unique constraint              | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0963  | upsert                         | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0964  | vacuum                         | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0965  | view                           | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                                          |
| 0966  | WAL                            | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0967  | warehouse model                | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0968  | write skew                     | Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words                 | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                                               |
| 0969  | ABAC                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0970  | access control                 | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0971  | account                        | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 0972  | ACL                            | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 0973  | allowlist                      | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0974  | attestation                    | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A proof that a thing is what it claims to be or was produced the way it claims. It matters most when trust cannot be assumed.                                                           |
| 0975  | audit                          | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0976  | authenticate                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | Prove who is making the claim before deciding what the claimant may do. Shadow: trusting names that have not earned entrance.                                                           |
| 0977  | authentication                 | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 0978  | authorization                  | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | After identity is known, what acts are permitted? Shadow: accidental omnipotence hidden behind a successful login.                                                                      |
| 0979  | availability                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A promise word that says the system can still be reached when called upon.                                                                                                              |
| 0980  | bearer token                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0981  | blast radius                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0982  | blocklist                      | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0983  | boundary defense               | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0984  | capability                     | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0985  | certificate                    | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A delegated-trust word; it lets one party assert another's identity by signed authority.                                                                                                |
| 0986  | challenge                      | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0987  | cipher                         | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0988  | claim                          | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0989  | client secret                  | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 0990  | code signing                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0991  | compliance                     | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0992  | confidentiality                | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0993  | credential                     | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0994  | cross-site scripting           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 0995  | cryptographic nonce            | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0996  | CSRF                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0997  | decrypt                        | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | An unsealing word; protected meaning becomes legible again under the right key.                                                                                                         |
| 0998  | defense in depth               | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 0999  | deny-by-default                | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1000  | digest                         | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1001  | enclave                        | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1002  | encrypt                        | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A warding word; it hides meaning from everyone except the right holder of the key.                                                                                                      |
| 1003  | entropy                        | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1004  | exfiltration                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1005  | exploit                        | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1006  | harden                         | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1007  | hash                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | Arbitrary input reduced to a fixed fingerprint. Use it for identity, integrity, bucketing, and change detection; never mistake it for secrecy.                                          |
| 1008  | HMAC                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1009  | identity                       | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1010  | incident                       | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1011  | injection                      | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 1012  | integrity                      | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 1013  | isolate                        | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1014  | key                            | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1015  | key exchange                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1016  | key rotation                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1017  | least privilege                | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | Give only the authority required for the present act, no more. It is one of the simplest words for reducing future regret.                                                              |
| 1018  | login                          | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1019  | mTLS                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1020  | mutual authentication          | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1021  | nonce                          | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 1022  | passkey                        | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1023  | password                       | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 1024  | patch                          | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A repair word; a bounded alteration is applied to an existing body.                                                                                                                     |
| 1025  | pepper                         | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1026  | permission                     | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1027  | phishing                       | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1028  | policy                         | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | The declared rule by which a class of cases is decided. Good policy reduces arbitrary judgment; bad policy merely freezes confusion.                                                    |
| 1029  | principal                      | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1030  | private key                    | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1031  | privilege escalation           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1032  | proof of possession            | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1033  | public key                     | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1034  | quarantine                     | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1035  | rate limiting                  | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1036  | replay attack                  | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1037  | revocation                     | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1038  | role                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | An invocation word; it frames what kind of actor the model should pretend to be.                                                                                                        |
| 1039  | root of trust                  | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 1040  | rotate                         | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A defense word; it shapes how systems resist misuse, theft, or forgery.                                                                                                                 |
| 1041  | salt                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1042  | sandbox                        | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A confinement word; actions are permitted only within a restricted space.                                                                                                               |
| 1043  | scope                          | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1044  | secret                         | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A guarded-value word meant to remain hidden except to the few who must know it.                                                                                                         |
| 1045  | secure enclave                 | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1046  | security boundary              | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A ward-word; it protects trust, secrecy, or authority.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1047  | session fixation               | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1048  | session token                  | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1049  | sign                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A sealing word; origin or integrity is bound cryptographically to content.                                                                                                              |
| 1050  | signature                      | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | Identity or intent made checkable through cryptographic proof. Useful wherever trust must survive distance and replay.                                                                  |
| 1051  | single sign-on                 | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1052  | spoofing                       | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1053  | SSRF                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1054  | threat model                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1055  | token                          | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A countable unit of text or symbol used for parsing, modeling, or encoding.                                                                                                             |
| 1056  | trust anchor                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A threshold word; it governs who may enter, know, or act.                                                                                                                               |
| 1057  | trust boundary                 | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1058  | validate                       | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A gatekeeping word; inputs or outputs are checked against an expected form.                                                                                                             |
| 1059  | verification                   | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A truth-checking word that asks not merely for output, but for justified output.                                                                                                        |
| 1060  | vulnerability                  | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1061  | ward                           | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1062  | webhook signing                | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1063  | zero trust                     | Security, Trust, and Warding Words                             | A trust-boundary word; it matters because compromise often hides inside ordinary-looking names.                                                                                         |
| 1064  | artifact                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1065  | assemble                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1066  | baseline                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1067  | binary                         | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1068  | branch                         | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A divergence word; execution or history splits into alternate paths.                                                                                                                    |
| 1069  | build                          | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge word; it turns source into artifact.                                                                                                                                            |
| 1070  | build cache                    | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1071  | build graph                    | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1072  | bundle                         | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1073  | canary                         | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A deliberately limited release whose job is to discover whether production reality agrees with the story told in staging.                                                               |
| 1074  | changelog                      | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1075  | cherry-pick                    | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1076  | CI                             | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1077  | codegen                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1078  | commit                         | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A sealing word; it makes a change part of durable history.                                                                                                                              |
| 1079  | compile                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A transmutation word; it transforms one language into another fit for execution.                                                                                                        |
| 1080  | container                      | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1081  | container image                | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1082  | continuous delivery            | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1083  | continuous integration         | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1084  | dependency                     | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1085  | dependency graph               | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1086  | deploy                         | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | The crossing from forge to world. Many good ideas become bad facts only after they are actually shipped.                                                                                |
| 1087  | deployment ring                | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1088  | diff                           | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A visible account of what changed between one state and another. Good diffs make review local; bad diffs turn truth into fog.                                                           |
| 1089  | distribution                   | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1090  | Dockerfile                     | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1091  | downgrade                      | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1092  | environment                    | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1093  | feature flag                   | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1094  | formatter                      | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1095  | hermetic build                 | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1096  | install                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1097  | installer                      | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1098  | issue tracker                  | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1099  | job runner                     | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1100  | label                          | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1101  | lint                           | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1102  | lockfile                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1103  | manifest                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1104  | merge                          | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A reconciliation word; separate lines of change are brought back together.                                                                                                              |
| 1105  | migration script               | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1106  | minifier                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1107  | package                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1108  | package manager                | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1109  | patch release                  | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1110  | pin                            | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1111  | pipeline                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.                                                                                                                                  |
| 1112  | precommit hook                 | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1113  | preview environment            | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1114  | promote                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1115  | publish                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1116  | rebase                         | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1117  | release                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1118  | release candidate              | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1119  | repository                     | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A custody word; an official place from which versions or entities are retrieved.                                                                                                        |
| 1120  | reproducible build             | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1121  | restore                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1122  | revision                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1123  | rollback                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A reversal word; the system returns toward an earlier trusted state.                                                                                                                    |
| 1124  | rollout                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1125  | scaffold                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1126  | semantic version               | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A history-and-release word; it determines how change is packaged and remembered.                                                                                                        |
| 1127  | ship                           | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1128  | source map                     | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
| 1129  | staging                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1130  | static analysis                | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1131  | tag                            | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1132  | template                       | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1133  | test gate                      | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1134  | toolchain                      | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1135  | transitive dependency          | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1136  | upgrade                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A forge-word; it turns work into artifact, revision, or release.                                                                                                                        |
| 1137  | version                        | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A chronology word; the same thing is named at different moments.                                                                                                                        |
| 1138  | version pin                    | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A delivery word; it moves software through build, review, and deployment.                                                                                                               |
| 1139  | workspace                      | Build, Tooling, Versioning, and Release                        | A workshop word; it shapes the rituals by which teams produce running systems.                                                                                                          |
