| 1431  | balanced                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1432  | batched                        | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1433  | bounded                        | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1434  | cache-friendly                 | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1435  | canonical                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1436  | causal                         | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1437  | composable                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1438  | concurrent                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1439  | configurable                   | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1440  | consistent                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1441  | convergent                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1442  | crash-safe                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1443  | declarative                    | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1444  | deduplicated                   | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1445  | deterministic                  | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | Given the same inputs, the same result should return. This is the antidote to drift, heisenbugs, and unreviewable behavior.                                                             |
| 1446  | durable                        | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1447  | eager                          | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1448  | elastic                        | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1449  | encrypted                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1450  | eventual                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1451  | exact                          | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1452  | exhaustive                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1453  | extensible                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1454  | fault-tolerant                 | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A resilience word; some breakage is expected and survivable.                                                                                                                            |
| 1455  | federated                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1456  | formal                         | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1457  | generic                        | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1458  | hardened                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1459  | hermetic                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1460  | high-availability              | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1461  | idempotent                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect. Shadow: duplicate charges, duplicate emails, and fear of recovery.                                           |
| 1462  | immutable                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | Once written, this thing does not change in place. Immutability buys clarity, replayability, and safer sharing at the cost of update convenience.                                       |
| 1463  | incremental                    | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1464  | indexed                        | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1465  | isolated                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1466  | lazy                           | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1467  | linearizable                   | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | Every operation behaves as though it took effect at one real instant visible to all observers. Use it rarely, but mean it fully.                                                        |
| 1468  | lock-free                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.                                                                                                                       |
| 1469  | lossless                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1470  | lossy                          | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1471  | maintainable                   | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1472  | memory-safe                    | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise that the system will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries. Shadow: leaks, corruption, and security bugs born from illegal touch.                                |
| 1473  | modular                        | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1474  | observable                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1475  | opaque                         | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1476  | orthogonal                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1477  | parallel                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1478  | portable                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1479  | predictable                    | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1480  | private                        | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1481  | pure                           | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | Same inputs, same output, no hidden state smuggled in from the side. Use it to shrink reasoning scope.                                                                                  |
| 1482  | reactive                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1483  | reentrant                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A safe-return word; the same code may be entered again before the first invocation finishes.                                                                                            |
| 1484  | reliable                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1485  | reproducible                   | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1486  | resilient                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1487  | responsive                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1488  | safe                           | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarded word; some class of failure is explicitly ruled out.                                                                                                                          |
| 1489  | sandboxed                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1490  | scalable                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1491  | secure                         | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1492  | serializable                   | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | Concurrency forced to behave as though transactions happened in one clean order. Expensive when overused, indispensable when history must remain sane.                                  |
| 1493  | sharded                        | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1494  | side-effect-free               | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1495  | signed                         | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1496  | stable                         | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1497  | stateful                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1498  | stateless                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1499  | streaming                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1500  | strict                         | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                                                 |
| 1501  | synchronized                   | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1502  | testable                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1503  | thread-safe                    | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1504  | traceable                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1505  | transactional                  | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1506  | typed                          | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1507  | validated                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1508  | vectorized                     | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1509  | verified                       | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                                                         |
| 1510  | versioned                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                                                    |
| 1511  | wait-free                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                                                             |
| 1512  | zero-copy                      | Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives | A nearness-and-efficiency word; data moves without redundant duplication.                                                                                                               |
| 1513  | anomaly                        | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1514  | backlog collapse               | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1515  | broken pipe                    | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1516  | bug                            | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1517  | cascading failure              | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1518  | checksum mismatch              | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1519  | collision                      | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1520  | contention                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1521  | corruption                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1522  | crash                          | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1523  | curse of shared state          | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1524  | data drift                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1525  | deadlock                       | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A binding curse; multiple actors wait on one another forever.                                                                                                                           |
| 1526  | defect                         | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1527  | degraded mode                  | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1528  | denial of service              | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1529  | dependency hell                | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1530  | divergence                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1531  | drift                          | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1532  | error                          | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1533  | exception                      | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1534  | exhaustion                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1535  | failure domain                 | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1536  | fault                          | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1537  | flapping                       | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1538  | fragmentation                  | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1539  | freeze                         | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1540  | hang                           | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1541  | hotspot                        | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1542  | inconsistent read              | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1543  | infinite loop                  | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1544  | invalid state                  | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1545  | jitter spike                   | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1546  | leak                           | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | Something escapes the boundary that should have held it: memory, secrets, file descriptors, authority, abstraction, or time.                                                            |
| 1547  | livelock                       | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1548  | lost update                    | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1549  | memory corruption              | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1550  | mismatch                       | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1551  | misroute                       | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1552  | missed heartbeat               | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1553  | null dereference               | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1554  | outage                         | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1555  | overflow                       | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1556  | packet loss                    | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1557  | panic                          | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1558  | partial failure                | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1559  | partition storm                | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1560  | poison pill                    | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1561  | race                           | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | An ordering curse: outcomes depend on timing you do not actually control. Name the race and the system becomes debuggable again.                                                        |
| 1562  | regression                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1563  | resource starvation            | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1564  | retry storm                    | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | Recovery logic amplifying failure instead of containing it. Unguided retries can turn a partial outage into a self-made siege.                                                          |
| 1565  | rollback failure               | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1566  | saturation                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1567  | schema drift                   | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | The stored world and the assumed world have silently stopped matching. This is how migrations keep hurting after everyone thinks they are done.                                         |
| 1568  | split-brain                    | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1569  | stack overflow                 | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1570  | stale read                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1571  | stall                          | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A curse-word; it names a bad state so it can stop masquerading as mystery.                                                                                                              |
| 1572  | starvation                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1573  | state explosion                | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1574  | storm                          | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1575  | thrash                         | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1576  | timeout                        | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A patience-bound word; after a duration, waiting is no longer allowed.                                                                                                                  |
| 1577  | torn write                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1578  | underflow                      | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1579  | unhandled exception            | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1580  | unstable equilibrium           | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A counter-spell word; it marks the shadow that disciplined engineering tries to prevent.                                                                                                |
| 1581  | write amplification            | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A failure-name; it gives shape to an otherwise vague loss of correctness or service.                                                                                                    |
| 1582  | write skew                     | Failure Words, Pathologies, and Counter-Spells                 | A pathology word; it describes how systems break, stall, or lie.                                                                                                                        |
| 1583  | -able                          | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1584  | -aware                         | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1585  | -by-design                     | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1586  | -centric                       | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1587  | -complete                      | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1588  | -driven                        | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1589  | -first                         | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1590  | -free                          | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1591  | -friendly                      | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1592  | -hard                          | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A naming rune; it changes meaning by attachment or compound form.                                                                                                                       |
| 1593  | -less                          | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A naming rune; it changes meaning by attachment or compound form.                                                                                                                       |
| 1594  | -local                         | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A naming rune; it changes meaning by attachment or compound form.                                                                                                                       |
| 1595  | -native                        | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A naming rune; it changes meaning by attachment or compound form.                                                                                                                       |
| 1596  | -of-record                     | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1597  | -oriented                      | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1598  | -safe                          | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A naming rune; it changes meaning by attachment or compound form.                                                                                                                       |
| 1599  | -sensitive                     | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1600  | -shaped                        | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1601  | -soft                          | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1602  | -tolerant                      | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1603  | -wide                          | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1604  | actor model                    | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1605  | API-first                      | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1606  | append-only                    | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1607  | best-effort                    | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1608  | branch-free                    | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1609  | cloud-native                   | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1610  | command-query                  | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1611  | control plane                  | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1612  | copy-on-write                  | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1613  | crash-only                     | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A naming rune; it changes meaning by attachment or compound form.                                                                                                                       |
| 1614  | data plane                     | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1615  | data-driven                    | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1616  | default-deny                   | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1617  | edge-native                    | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
| 1618  | event-driven                   | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1619  | eventually consistent          | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1620  | fail-fast                      | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1621  | first-class                    | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1622  | hot path                       | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A naming rune; it changes meaning by attachment or compound form.                                                                                                                       |
| 1623  | infrastructure-as-code         | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1624  | least-authority                | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A semantic affix or compound; it compresses a recurring engineering pattern into a compact mark.                                                                                        |
| 1625  | lock-free                      | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.                                                                                                                       |
| 1626  | machine-readable               | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A pattern rune; its power comes from how it modifies neighboring terms.                                                                                                                 |
| 1627  | memory-safe                    | Compound Forms, Prefixes, Suffixes, and Naming Runes           | A compositional word-form; it carries repeated design intent across many contexts.                                                                                                      |
